items.title: Lockheed Martin unveils baby Patriot missile to address urgent gap
items.preview: Ukraine is begging for more anti-ballistic missile interceptors, and other allies want them too.

items.title: Can new drones, 3D printers defeat distance’s tyranny? RIMPAC aims to find out
items.preview: “This is Uber for manufacturing delivered at the speed of Amazon, for the highest quality of parts,” Marine Lt. Col. Michael Radigan told reporters.

items.title: How a former Marine is rewriting the future of battlefield AI
items.preview: A pilot program in the Pacific is showing how to get advanced tools all the way to the edge.

items.title: AI can now power every stage of a cyberattack
items.preview: Researchers determined AI has been used to identify security flaws, generate commands, and carry out parts of intrusions.

items.title: How Ukraine won the first great robot war
items.preview: In a new video, Science & Tech editor Patrick Tucker looks at how the narrative has shifted.

items.title: Lightweight cells powered by nuclear waste could drive tomorrow's drones
items.preview: DARPA program aims to create a 30-year battery minimally viable prototype by early 2027.

items.title: GenAI.mil records almost 1.7M users, plans new model additions
items.preview: “It's just a really exciting time for generative AI in the department,” the Pentagon’s chief artificial intelligence officer said.

items.title: Pentagon recruiting new tech talent for AI implementation
items.preview: “War Force” of young two-year recruits will get access to “policymaking and national-scale impact,” according to the announcement.

items.title: Pentagon launches ‘War Force’ initiative to onboard tech talent
items.preview: The new recruitment effort operates under the Office of Personnel Management's larger Tech Force program.

items.title: Army using AI, robot boats for Pacific logistics
items.preview: “If you can work in the Pacific, you can work anywhere in the world,” said Maj. Gen. Gavin Gardner.

items.title: Agentic-AI tools aim to give US commanders new target options ‘within seconds’
items.preview: But concerns persist about the power and governance of software agents.

items.title: Could the Army’s light squad vehicle power battlefield drones?
items.preview: A mobile brigade in the 101st Airborne Division put drones to the test in a recent training rotation—and used the Infantry Squad Vehicle to keep unmanned…

items.title: RIMPAC kicks off in Hawaii with a focus on experimentation
items.preview: The theme of this edition of the biennial exercise is “partners: integrated and prepared.”

items.title: Executive orders seek to hasten quantum computing—and guard against its use
items.preview: One directs a "national effort" toward a useful quantum computer; the other sets deadlines for quantum-resistant encryption.

items.title: CISA now has full Mythos Preview access, people familiar say
items.preview: The cyberdefense agency got access to the model about a week ago, but remains without clear guidance from the White House.
